CHARTS / BASKETS
% RETURN, REBASED · EOD 2026-07-21
DEFENSIVES LEADCyclicals -3.0% vs defensives on the month
Rotation ReadCitrini · claude

The rotation is rotating right out of AI infrastructure and into healthcare, financials, and software. XLV leads sectors at +5.79% one-month and XLF follows at +4.42%, while XLK has bled -4.78% over the same window despite holding +23.02% year-to-date. Cyber is the standout theme at +20.9% one-month and +57.1% YTD, with S leading and FTNT lagging, and the software basket at +12.6% one-month confirms capital is chasing secular-growth names that missed the first AI leg. The GLP-1 basket at +13.0% one-month with VKTX leading tells you the healthcare bid has a thematic engine under it, and biotech at +7.6% with MRNA out front broadens that read past pure pharma.

The central tension is whether this is healthy broadening or the start of a risk-off rotation disguised as sector strength. Energy at +24.91% YTD and XLE at +3.0% one-month says cyclicals still have bid, and XLI at +14.03% YTD supports that. Semis at -8.5% one-month and AI infra at -9.9% one-month, both still massively positive YTD at +67.1% and +78.1%, look like profit-taking in the market's most crowded longs. Gold miners at -17.0% one-month, uranium at -18.7%, and quantum at -32.4% confirm the risk is coming out of the speculative end. SPY/TLT at a z-score of 2.14, in the top 2% of its yearly range, and SPY/IEF at 2.02 in the top 4% show equities remain extreme versus duration, and HYG/LQD at 2.08 in the top 1% of its range tells you high-yield credit is still compressing spreads against investment grade. That credit signal confirms the risk-on read for now. IWF/IWD at -1.86, in the bottom 1% of its yearly range, is the tell that growth leadership is cracking relative to value, and you should be rotating into the XLV and XLF bid rather than fading it as a head-fake. The read flips if semis stabilize and AI infra stops making new one-month lows while XLV rolls over.

AI infrastructure is rolling over while healthcare, financials, and software absorb the capital, and credit spreads are still tight enough to call it rotation rather than retreat.

BULL

XLF at +4.42% one-month, energy at +30.7% YTD theme-level, and HYG/LQD at 2.08 in the top 1% of its range confirm cyclical risk appetite is intact. Banks at +4.0% one-month with PNC leading tells you the financial bid has earnings-season fuel.

BEAR

Semis at -8.5%, AI infra at -9.9%, and Mag 7 at only +2.4% one-month with GOOGL lagging signal the market's largest weights are weakening. IWF/IWD in the bottom 1% of its yearly range confirms growth-to-value rotation is already underway.

TELL

IWF/IWD at -1.86 is the ratio to watch. If it stabilizes here, the rotation is controlled broadening. If it keeps falling through the bottom 1% of its range, you have a growth drawdown accelerating into a market-wide event.

⚠ Energy at **+24.91%** YTD and XLE at **+3.0%** one-month signal cyclical strength and risk-on positioning. vs Gold miners at **-17.0%** one-month and uranium at **-18.7%** one-month signal commodity complex stress and risk-off under the surface.: The conflict is between traditional energy as a cyclical leader and the speculative commodity complex collapsing. Trust the energy read because XLE's strength aligns with HYG/LQD at **2.08** in the top **1%** of its range, while gold miners and uranium are idiosyncratic drawdowns in overheated names that overshot earlier.
⚠ XLV at **+5.79%** one-month and XLF at **+4.42%** one-month signal defensive and value rotation into healthcare and financials. vs HYG/LQD at **2.08** in the top **1%** of its range and SPY/IEF at **2.02** in the top **4%** signal aggressive risk-on positioning in credit and equities over duration.: Healthcare and financials leading is typically a defensive signal, but tight high-yield spreads say risk appetite is intact. Trust the credit signal because HYG/LQD leads equities, and the sector rotation into XLV and XLF is broadening, not retreating.

URA/SPY at -2.44 in the bottom 1% of its yearly range is the most extreme stretched ratio on the board and a clean mean-reversion candidate given uranium's -18.7% one-month collapse and -27.5% YTD drawdown. XRT/XLY at 2.35 in the top 1% of its range is stretched to the upside and confirmed trend, not a reversion trade, because XLY at -0.95% one-month and -0.85% YTD is genuinely weak while consumer staples at +10.45% YTD and retail strength persist.

SPY/TLT at 2.14 in the top 2% and TLT/SPY at -2.03 in the bottom 2% are mirror images of the same extreme, and both are confirmed trend as long as HYG/LQD at 2.08 in the top 1% holds. The credit and cross-asset ratios are telling you the same thing: equities over bonds is stretched but not reversing until spreads widen.

EMB/TLT at 1.94 in the top 3% and AGG/TLT at 1.77 in the top 2% reinforce that duration is hated across the board, and that is a crowded short worth respecting but not fighting yet. XLC/SPY at -1.97 in the bottom 9% of its range and XLY/SPY at -1.84 in the bottom 7% are confirmed trend, not reversion, because communications and consumer discretionary are fundamental laggards with XLC at -3.64% YTD and XLY negative on both timeframes.
